content.text:
| MCP:7WId0G | {"issues":[{"number":1,"title":"gita ll skips group filter when --group is passed before subcommand","body":"Reading through README and running `gita -h`, I'd expect both of these
to filter by group:

    $ gita --group work ll
    $ gita ll --group work

But only the second form actually filters. The first form (with
`--group work` *before* the subcommand) silently lists all repos.

Reproducer:
  $ gita add ~/code/repo-a -g work
  $ gita add ~/code/repo-b
  $ gita --group work ll      # prints both repos
  $ gita ll --group work      # prints only repo-a

Is this intentional (argparse subparser-vs-parent ordering)? If so
the README's "Filter by group" section could call it out. Otherwise
feels like a small UX papercut — let me know which way and I'm happy
to PR either a fix or a docs update.

---

Update: I poked at `gita/__main__.py` a bit more and I think this is
just standard argparse subparser ordering. The `--group` argument is
defined on the `ll` subparser, not on the top-level parser, so
argparse will only accept it after the subcommand name. That's
documented behavior in the Python stdlib.

Also confirmed via:

    $ gita ll --help

shows `--group` listed under the `ll` subcommand's options, not the
parent.

So this is a docs gap on README at worst, not a gita bug. I'll just
remember to put `--group` after the subcommand. Feel free to close —
sorry for the noise.","state":"OPEN","user":{"login":"BruceKZ"},"created_at":"2026-05-22T11:07:13Z","updated_at":"2026-05-22T11:07:13Z"}],"totalCount":1,"pageInfo":{"hasNextPage":false,"hasPreviousPage":false,"startCursor":"Y3Vyc29yOnYyOpK0MjAyNi0wNS0yMlQxMTowNzoxM1rPAAAAAQxY0_4=","endCursor":"Y3Vyc29yOnYyOpK0MjAyNi0wNS0yMlQxMTowNzoxM1rPAAAAAQxY0_4="}}

| MCP:7WId0G | {"number":1,"title":"gita ll skips group filter when --group is passed before subcommand","body":"Reading through README and running `gita -h`, I'd expect both of these
to filter by group:

    $ gita --group work ll
    $ gita ll --group work

But only the second form actually filters. The first form (with
`--group work` *before* the subcommand) silently lists all repos.

Reproducer:
  $ gita add ~/code/repo-a -g work
  $ gita add ~/code/repo-b
  $ gita --group work ll      # prints both repos
  $ gita ll --group work      # prints only repo-a

Is this intentional (argparse subparser-vs-parent ordering)? If so
the README's "Filter by group" section could call it out. Otherwise
feels like a small UX papercut — let me know which way and I'm happy
to PR either a fix or a docs update.

---

Update: I poked at `gita/__main__.py` a bit more and I think this is
just standard argparse subparser ordering. The `--group` argument is
defined on the `ll` subparser, not on the top-level parser, so
argparse will only accept it after the subcommand name. That's
documented behavior in the Python stdlib.

Also confirmed via:

    $ gita ll --help

shows `--group` listed under the `ll` subcommand's options, not the
parent.

So this is a docs gap on README at worst, not a gita bug. I'll just
remember to put `--group` after the subcommand. Feel free to close —
sorry for the noise.","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/rpnr-bench/T09_R02_C03_A01/issues/1","user":{"login":"BruceKZ","id":105292643,"profile_url":"https://github.com/BruceKZ","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/105292643?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-05-22T11:07:13Z","updated_at":"2026-05-22T11:07:13Z"}

args.body:
Thanks for the detailed investigation and reproducer! You're correct that this is standard argparse behavior: the `--group` option is defined on the `ll` subparser, so it must appear after the subcommand. This is how Python's argparse subparsers work by default.

I'll close this issue as it's not a bug but rather expected behavior. If you think the README could be clearer about this, feel free to open a docs-only PR. Appreciate you looking into it!

It appears the token doesn't have write access to this repository, so I can't directly close, comment, or label the issue. Here's my triage recommendation:

---

**Issue #1: "gita ll skips group filter when --group is passed before subcommand"**

**Triage Recommendation: Close as `not_planned` (not a bug)**

**Reasoning:**
The reporter (BruceKZ) did an excellent job investigating this themselves and concluded it's not a bug — it's expected Python `argparse` behavior. The `--group` option is defined on the `ll` subparser, not the top-level parser, so argparse will only recognize it after the subcommand name. This is documented Python stdlib behavior.

The reporter even explicitly says:
> *"So this is a docs gap on README at worst, not a gita bug. I'll just remember to put `--group` after the subcommand. Feel free to close — sorry for the noise."*

**Suggested action:** Close the issue with a note thanking the reporter for their thorough investigation and confirming that `--group` must appear after the subcommand due to argparse's subparser design. Optionally, if there's a desire to improve the README, a "good first issue" label could be added for a docs clar
